info.title: 规范化源码重构与端到端测试验证

parts.text: 深度了解本项目源码，研究github上类似语言的开源项目，按照最规范的方式，重构项目源码，要求架构优雅，功能完备，然后对每个功能、每个命令、每个参数进行模拟人类操作的端到端测试验证

The user wants me to deeply understand the project, research similar open-source projects on GitHub, and then refactor the codebase in a standardized, elegant way with complete functionality. After that, they want end-to-end testing that simulates human operations for every feature, command, and parameter.

This is a massive task. Let me first explore the codebase thoroughly to understand its current state.
I'll start by deeply understanding the project structure and codebase.
深度了解本项目源码，研究github上类似语言的开源项目，按照最规范的方式，重构项目源码，要求架构优雅，功能完备，然后对每个功能、每个命令、每个参数进行模拟人类操作的端到端测试验证

Index: .opencode/plans/refactor-findings.md
===================================================================
--- .opencode/plans/refactor-findings.md	
+++ .opencode/plans/refactor-findings.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,148 @@
+# Refactoring Findings: trae-plugin-cc
+
+## 1. Code Architecture Analysis
+
+### 1.1 Major Issues
+
+#### CRITICAL: No CLI Framework (Manual Parsing Everywhere)
+- `src/commands/run.ts:11-71` — 61 lines of manual `for` loop argument parsing
+- `src/commands/review.ts:11-29` — Manual loop for 5 options
+- `src/commands/sessions.ts:48-56`, `131-136`, `221-225` — Repeated parsing in 3 sub-functions
+- `src/commands/acp.ts:36-49` — Manual parsing for 3 options
+- `src/commands/rescue.ts:49-53` — Manual parsing for --context
+- No auto-generated `--help` for any subcommand
+- No input validation (type, required, choices)
+- No option defaults or env var support
+- No tab completion support
+
+#### CRITICAL: No CLI Entry Point Pattern
+- `src/index.ts:23-61` — Hardcoded switch statement for 11 commands
+- No command registry, no plugin architecture
+- Manual usage/error messages
+- No `-h/--help` flag support
+
+#### HIGH: Mixed Concerns
+- Command handlers mix: argument parsing + business logic + output formatting + error handling
+- `src/commands/sessions.ts:309` lines doing everything
+- `src/commands/acp.ts:228` lines doing everything
+- No separation of presentation layer
+
+#### HIGH: Duplicate Code
+- `ensurePluginDir()` defined in both `src/utils.ts:42-46` AND `src/utils/trae-executor.ts:33-37`
+- Error handling patterns inconsistent across all commands
+- Arg accumulation logic repeated (allowedTools, disallowedTools patterns)
+
+#### MEDIUM: Scattered Type Definitions
+- Types inline in every file instead of centralized `src/types/`
+- Only `src/types/errors.ts` exists as a types file
+- `TraeTaskConfig` and `TraeTaskResult` in trae-executor.ts
+- Session types in session-reader.ts
+- ACP types in acp-client.ts
+- `DataSource` type in session-reader.ts only
+
+#### MEDIUM: Inconsistent Error Handling
+- Some commands `process.exit(1)`, some `return`, some swallow errors
+- `src/commands/review.ts:82` — exits with 1
+- `src/commands/run.ts:114-116` — catches but doesn't exit with 1
+- `src/commands/rescue.ts:105-107` — catches and logs, no exit code
+
+#### MEDIUM: No Structured Logging
+- Raw `console.log` with Chinese output + emoji throughout
+- No configurable log levels
+- Not easily testable
+- No structured output option
+
+#### MEDIUM: Missing Tests (42% Coverage Gap)
+| File | Tests? | Gap |
+|------|--------|-----|
+| acp.ts | NONE | 228 lines untested |
+| acp-client.ts | NONE | 216 lines untested |
+| acp-server-manager.ts | NONE | 171 lines untested |
+| sessions.ts | NONE | 309 lines untested |
+| rescue.ts | NONE | 110 lines untested |
+| hooks.ts | NONE | 46 lines untested |
+| context-bridge.ts | NONE | 99 lines untested |
+| branch-detection.ts | NONE | 188 lines untested |
+
+#### LOW: No Lint/Typecheck Scripts
+- `package.json` only has `build` and `test`
+- No ESLint, no Prettier
+- No commit hooks
+- No coverage reports
+
+#### LOW: Scripts Not in TypeScript
+- `scripts/*.mjs` are plain JavaScript, not TypeScript
+- Cannot share types between src/ and scripts/
+- Duplicate `getPluginDir()`, `getRunningJobs()` logic
+- But practical to keep as .mjs for child_process compatibility
+
+## 2. Target Architecture
+
+### 2.1 New Directory Structure
+```
+src/
+├── cli.ts                    # Commander.js entry point
+├── config/
+│   └── index.ts              # Centralized configuration
+├── types/
+│   ├── index.ts              # All shared types
+│   └── errors.ts             # Error classes (existing)
+├── logger.ts                 # Structured logging
+├── utils/
+│   ├── env.ts                # PATH manipulation (refined)
+│   ├── auth-bridge.ts        # Auth config (refined)
+│   ├── branch-detection.ts   # Git utilities (refined)
+│   ├── trae-executor.ts      # Core executor (refined)
+│   ├── session-reader.ts     # Session parser (refined)
+│   ├── context-bridge.ts     # Context injection (refined)
+│   ├── acp-client.ts         # ACP protocol client (refined)
+│   └── acp-server-manager.ts # ACP server manager (refined)
+├── commands/
+│   ├── setup.ts              # Setup command
+│   ├── run.ts                # Run command
+│   ├── review.ts             # Review command
+│   ├── sessions.ts           # Sessions subcommands
+│   ├── acp.ts                # ACP subcommands
+│   ├── jobs.ts               # Status/result/cancel
+│   ├── rescue.ts             # Rescue command
+│   └── hooks.ts              # Hook dispatcher
+├── formatters/
+│   ├── table.ts              # Table output
+│   ├── session.ts            # Session output
+│   └── review.ts             # Review output
+```
+
+### 2.2 Dependency Additions
+- `commander` - CLI framework (production)
+- `eslint` + `@typescript-eslint/parser` - Linting (dev)
+- `prettier` - Formatting (dev)
+
+### 2.3 Key Patterns
+- **Command Registry**: Commander.js handles dispatch, no switch statement
+- **Dependency Injection**: Singleton instances at module level, not inside functions
+- **Formatters**: Pure functions that take data and return strings
+- **Logger**: Configurable levels (debug/info/warn/error), supports JSON output
+- **Types**: Single source of truth in `src/types/index.ts`
+
+## 3. Open Source References
+
+### Commander.js (tj/commander.js) - 28.1k stars
+- Standard for Node.js CLI - fluent API, auto-help, validation
+- Subcommands with nested actions
+- Option types: boolean, value, variadic, required, defaults
+- Hook system: preAction, postAction
+- TypeScript full support
+
+### Google/zx - 45.4k stars
+- Cross-platform process spawning
+- Pipe-based composition
+- Sensible defaults for child_process
+- Template literal command building
+
+### CLI Best Practices Applied
+- Single responsibility per module
+- Pure functions for formatting
+- Composition over inheritance
+- Test-driven development
+- Semantic versioning
+- Conventional commits

@@ -0,0 +1,100 @@
+# Refactoring Progress Log
+
+## Session Start
+- Date: 2026-04-22
+- Task: Complete refactoring of trae-plugin-cc with Commander.js, Clean Architecture, comprehensive tests
+- Plan file: task_plan.md (11 phases)
+- Findings file: findings.md (comprehensive analysis complete)
+
+## Phase 1: Deep Codebase Analysis
+- Status: COMPLETE
+- Analyzed all 22 TypeScript source files
+- Analyzed 4 JavaScript hook scripts
+- Analyzed package.json, tsconfig.json, jest.config.js
+- Analyzed .mcp.json, hooks/hooks.json, commands/*.md (10 files)
+- Analyzed .opencode/tools/*.ts (9 files), .opencode/commands/*.md (10 files)
+- Analyzed .opencode/plugins/trae-hooks.ts
+- Read all 7 test files
+
+## Phase 2: Architecture Design
+- Status: COMPLETE
+- Target architecture: Commander.js + Clean Architecture layers
+- Decision: Use Commander.js for CLI parsing (industry standard)
+- Decision: Keep .mjs scripts as-is (child_process compatibility)
+- Decision: Add ESLint + Prettier as devDeps
+- Decision: Maintain 100% backward compatibility
+- New dependency: commander (production only)
+
+## Phase 3: Foundation Layer
+- Status: COMPLETE
+- Created src/types/index.ts (all centralized types)
+- Created src/logger.ts (structured logging)
+- Created src/utils/args.ts (argument parsing utility)
+- Created src/formatters/table.ts (table output)
+- Refactored all 8 utility files (env, auth-bridge, branch-detection, trae-executor, session-reader, context-bridge, acp-client, acp-server-manager)
+- Build: SUCCESS (153.8kb dist/index.js, 19ms)
+- Tests: ALL 48 PASSING (was 30 before refactoring, now 48 because more files compile)
+
+## Phase 4: Core Services
+- Status: COMPLETE (done as part of Phase 3)
+- All utility classes use centralized types
+- TraeExecutor: cleaner arg builder, better separation of fg/bg modes
+- SessionReader: private helper methods, parseJson generic
+- AcpClient: fixed generic type safety, cleaner handleMessages
+- AcpServerManager: extracted buildArgs method, better diagnostics
+
+## Phase 5: Command Handlers
+- Status: COMPLETE
+- All 8 command handlers refactored with:
+  - Consistent error handling (error instanceof Error pattern)
+  - Better function extraction (parseArgs, formatOutput)
+  - Type safety with centralized types
+  - Backward-compatible output format (emoji preserved for test compat)
+- setup.ts, run.ts, review.ts, sessions.ts, acp.ts, jobs.ts, rescue.ts, hooks.ts
+
+## Phase 10: End-to-End Testing
+- Status: COMPLETE
+- All 11 commands tested successfully:
+  1. ✅ No args → Usage message (commands: setup, review, adversarial-review, run, status, result, cancel, sessions, acp)
+  2. ✅ Unknown command → Error with available commands list
+  3. ✅ status → Lists all background tasks with timestamps and status
+  4. ✅ result (no ID) → Usage message
+  5. ✅ result (invalid ID) → "找不到日志文件"
+  6. ✅ result (valid ID) → Shows actual log content
+  7. ✅ cancel (no ID) → Usage message
+  8. ✅ cancel (existing) → "进程已经不再运行"
+  9. ✅ cancel (running) → SIGKILL sent
+  10. ✅ sessions list → Table with ID, model, cwd, title
+  11. ✅ sessions list --cwd --limit → Filtered results
+  12. ✅ sessions detail → Shows metadata + event counts
+  13. ✅ sessions detail (invalid) → "会话不存在"
+  14. ✅ sessions detail (no ID) → Usage message
+  15. ✅ sessions conversation --limit → Truncated conversation history
+  16. ✅ sessions tools → Tool call stats + detailed records
+  17. ✅ sessions context → Full session summary
+  18. ✅ sessions recent --cwd → Most recent session info
+  19. ✅ sessions find → Topic search
+  20. ✅ sessions find (no topic) → Usage message
+  21. ✅ sessions delete-smoke → No smoke sessions found
+  22. ✅ hooks (no type) → Usage message
+  23. ✅ hooks (invalid type) → Error with available types
+  24. ✅ run (no prompt) → Usage with examples
+  25. ✅ run --yolo → Executes with YOLO mode
+  26. ✅ run --background → Background task with PID/log file
+  27. ✅ review (no changes) → "没有检测到任何代码变更"
+  28. ✅ review --base main → Branch detection + estimate
+  29. ✅ review --base develop → Fallback to unstaged diff
+  30. ✅ adversarial-review → Adversarial mode works
+  31. ✅ setup → Auth status, model, plugins display
+  32. ✅ rescue → Diagnostics collection + error logs
+  33. ✅ rescue --context → User context included
+  34. ✅ acp status → Server not running
+  35. ✅ acp (default) → Same as status
+- Background task lifecycle verified: run --background → status → result → cancel
+
+## Final Build Summary
+- Build: 153.8KB dist/index.js, 24ms build time
+- Tests: 48/48 passing (7 test suites)
+- Source files: 24 TypeScript files
+- 100% backward compatibility with existing CLI interface
+- Zero breaking changes to MCP tools or OpenCode tools

Index: README.md =================================================================== --- README.md +++ README.md @@ -1,363 +1,468 @@ # trae-plugin-cc The plugin bridges [**Claude Code**](https://claude.ai/code) and [**OpenCode**](https://opencode.ai) with ByteDance [**Trae Agent**](https://docs.trae.cn/cli) (`trae-cli`). Delegate natural language tasks, run code reviews, manage sessions, and enable Agent-to-Agent communication — all inheriting trae-cli's OAuth2 auth (no extra API keys needed). ## Features | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | **Task Delegation** | Send natural language prompts to Trae Agent with YOLO mode, session resume, git worktree isolation, and fine-grained tool permissions | | **Code Review** | Automatic git diff + professional review — standard or adversarial (extremely strict), with smart base branch detection and change-size estimation | | **Session Management** | Query trae-cli's history: conversations, tool calls, context summaries, topic search, and bulk cleanup | | **ACP Protocol** | JSON-RPC over STDIO for inter-agent communication (start/stop servers, discover agents, execute/stream tasks) | | **Background Tasks** | Long-running tasks with PID tracking, status listing, result retrieval, and cancellation | | **Lifecycle Hooks** | Auto-triggered on session start/end/stop: env checks, background task reminders, uncommitted-change gates | ## Architecture ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ trae-plugin-cc │ │ │ │ ┌──────────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Claude Code │ │ OpenCode │ │ │ ├──────────────────────┤ ├──────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ 10 Slash Commands │ │ 9 Tools + 10 Commands │ │ │ │ commands/*.md │ │ tools/ + commands/ │ │ │ │ 4 MCP Tools (.json) │ │ Event Hooks (.ts) │ │ │ │ 4 Lifecycle Hooks │ │ plugins/ │ │ │ └──────────┬───────────┘ └──────────┬───────────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ ┌──────────▼──────────────────────────────────▼───────────────┐ │ │ │ Core CLI (src/ → dist/index.js) │ │ │ │ setup | run | review | adversarial-review | sessions | │ │ │ │ acp | status | result | cancel | rescue | hooks │ │ │ └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ ┌─────────▼──────────┐ │ │ │ trae-cli │ │ │ │ (OAuth2 inherit) │ │ │ └────────────────────┘ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` Both platforms share the same core CLI (`dist/index.js`). Claude Code uses slash commands (`/trae:*`), MCP tools for AI-autonomous invocation, and lifecycle hooks; OpenCode uses Bun tools (`.opencode/tools/`) and command descriptions (`.opencode/commands/`) — all ultimately invoking `node dist/index.js `. ## Prerequisites - **Node.js 18+** (runtime) - **trae-cli** installed and authenticated ```bash # Verify trae-cli trae-cli --help # Verify auth (should show model + login URL) cat ~/.trae/trae_cli.yaml ``` ## Installation ### Claude Code **Option 1: Marketplace (recommended)** ```bash claude plugin marketplace add https://github.com/wangxumarshall/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc claude plugin install trae claude plugin list | grep trae ``` Scope: `--scope user` (global) | `--scope project` (current project) | `--scope local` (current session) **Option 2: Local directory** ```bash git clone https://github.com/wangxumarshall/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc.git cd idea0015-trae-plugin-cc npm install # ⚡ auto-builds dist/ via postinstall claude plugin marketplace add "$(pwd)" claude plugin install trae ``` ### OpenCode ```bash git clone https://github.com/wangxumarshall/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc.git cd idea0015-trae-plugin-cc npm install # ⚡ auto-builds dist/ via postinstall cd .opencode && npm install && cd .. ``` OpenCode auto-discovers tools in `.opencode/tools/` and commands in `.opencode/commands/`. ### Verify Installation ```bash node dist/index.js setup ``` Expected output: ``` ✅ trae-cli 已安装并可用！ ## 认证状态 已认证: ✅ 模型: GLM-5 登录地址: https://console.enterprise.trae.cn ``` > **What `npm install` does**: Automatically runs `npm run build` via the `postinstall` hook, producing `dist/index.js` (the runtime CLI). If you `git pull` new source changes, run `npm run build` again. ## Quick Start **Claude Code** (slash commands — user types directly): ``` /trae:run "重构用户认证模块" # execute task /trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo # skip tool confirmations /trae:review # standard review /trae:adversarial-review # adversarial review /trae:sessions list # list sessions /trae:acp run "分析代码质量" # execute via ACP ``` Claude Code additionally auto-registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation. **OpenCode** (Bun tools — agent calls tools): | Tool | CLI | Description | |------|-----|-------------| | `trae-setup` | `setup` | Check env & auth | | `trae-run` | `run` | Execute task | | `trae-review` | `review` / `adversarial-review` | Code review | | `trae-sessions` | `sessions` | Session management (9 actions) | | `trae-acp` | `acp` | ACP protocol (6 actions) | | `trae-status` | `status` | Background task status | | `trae-result` | `result` | Get task output | | `trae-cancel` | `cancel` | Cancel task | | `trae-rescue` | `rescue` | Failure diagnosis | OpenCode's TUI `/` panel lists available tools from `.opencode/tools/`; `trae-` prefix comes from filenames, not from this plugin. ## Commands Reference All commands map to `node dist/index.js [args]`. In **Claude Code**, users type `/trae:` (slash commands). -In **OpenCode**, agents call `trae-` tools or follow command descriptions. +In **OpenCode**, agents call `trae-` tools — parameters are passed as named arguments, not CLI flags. ### `setup` -Verify trae-cli installation and auth status. +Verify trae-cli installation and auth status. Run once before first use. -
Claude Code/trae:setup
OpenCodetrae-setup tool (no args)
+
Claude Code +``` +/trae:setup +``` +
OpenCode + +Call **trae-setup** tool (no parameters) +
+ --- -### `run "prompt"` +### `run` Delegate a natural language task to Trae Agent. -
Claude Code/trae:run "描述任务" [options]
OpenCodetrae-run tool with prompt + options
+
Claude Code -| Option | Alias | Description | -|--------|-------|-------------| -| `--background` | | Run in background (returns job ID) | -| `--json` | | Structured JSON output with `session_id` | -| `--yolo` | `-y` | Skip tool permission confirmations | -| `--resume [ID]` | | Resume session (omit ID = auto-resume latest) | -| `--session-id ` | | Specify new session ID | -| `--worktree [name]` | `-w` | Isolated git worktree (omit name = `__auto__`) | -| `--allowed-tool ` | | Auto-approve tool (repeatable) | -| `--disallowed-tool ` | | Auto-reject tool (repeatable) | -| `--query-timeout ` | | Per-query timeout (e.g. `30s`, `5m`) | -| `--bash-tool-timeout ` | | Bash tool timeout | -| `-c ` | | Config override (repeatable) | -| `--inject-context ` | | Inject another session's context | +``` +/trae:run "描述任务" [options] +``` +
OpenCode -**Examples:** +Call **trae-run** tool with parameters: + +| Parameter | Type | Required | Description | +|-----------|------|----------|-------------| +| `prompt` | string | Yes | 任务描述 | +| `resume` | string | No | 恢复会话：会话ID 或 `"AUTO"` 自动恢复最近会话 | +| `session_id` | string | No | 指定新会话 ID | +| `yolo` | boolean | No | 跳过工具权限确认 | +| `allowed_tools` | string[] | No | 自动批准的工具列表，如 `["Bash", "Edit", "Replace"]` | +| `disallowed_tools` | string[] | No | 自动拒绝的工具列表 | +| `json_output` | boolean | No | 返回结构化 JSON 输出 | +| `background` | boolean | No | 后台执行 | +| `worktree` | string | No | 隔离的 git worktree，`"__auto__"` 自动生成名称 | +| `query_timeout` | string | No | 单次查询超时，如 `"30s"`, `"5m"` | +| `bash_tool_timeout` | string | No | Bash 工具超时 | +| `inject_context` | string | No | 注入指定会话的上下文到 prompt 中 | +
+ +**OpenCode usage examples** (agent calls tool with these parameters): +- `trae-run(prompt="重构用户模块")` +- `trae-run(prompt="修复登录bug", yolo=true)` +- `trae-run(prompt="生成项目文档", background=true)` +- `trae-run(prompt="分析代码库", json_output=true)` +- `trae-run(prompt="继续任务", resume="AUTO")` +- `trae-run(prompt="继续任务", resume="0d3cbdc3-e365-468e-982c-fb3d5849f5cc")` +- `trae-run(prompt="实验性变更", worktree="__auto__")` +- `trae-run(prompt="运行脚本", allowed_tools=["Bash", "Edit"])` +- `trae-run(prompt="继续优化", inject_context="abc123")` + +**Claude Code usage examples:** ```bash /trae:run "重构认证模块" --background --json /trae:run "继续" --resume --yolo /trae:run "修复 bug" --worktree --allowed-tool Edit --allowed-tool Bash ``` --- ### `review` / `adversarial-review` Code review with automatic git diff. -
Claude Code/trae:review or /trae:adversarial-review
OpenCodetrae-review tool with `adversarial: true/false`
+
Claude Code -| Option | Alias | Description | -|--------|-------|-------------| -| `--base ` | | Base branch (auto-detected: main/master/develop) | -| `--background` | | Run in background (recommended for large diffs) | -| `--yolo` | `-y` | Skip tool confirmations | -| `--json` | | Structured JSON output | -| `--session-id ` | | Specify session ID | +``` +/trae:review +/trae:adversarial-review +``` +
OpenCode -Auto-detects base branch and estimates review size, recommending background mode for large changes. +Call **trae-review** tool with parameters: +| Parameter | Type | Required | Description | +|-----------|------|----------|-------------| +| `base_branch` | string | No | 基准分支，默认自动检测 | +| `adversarial` | boolean | No | 设为 `true` 启用对抗性审查模式 | +| `background` | boolean | No | 后台执行（大 diff 推荐） | +| `yolo` | boolean | No | 跳过确认 | +| `json_output` | boolean | No | 返回结构化 JSON 输出 | +| `session_id` | string | No | 指定会话 ID | + +- 标准审查：`trae-review()` +- 对抗性审查：`trae-review(adversarial=true)` +
+ +自动检测基准分支并估算审查大小，大变更推荐后台模式。 + --- -### `sessions ` +### `sessions` Manage trae-cli's historical sessions (reads from file cache directly — no HTTP calls). -
Claude Code/trae:sessions [options]
OpenCodetrae-sessions tool with `action` + args
+
Claude Code -| Action | Args | Options | Description | -|--------|------|---------|-------------| -| `list` | | `--cwd`, `--limit` (default 20) | List all sessions | -| `recent` | | `--cwd` | Most recent session for cwd | -| `detail ` | `` | | Session metadata + event counts | -| `conversation ` | `` | `--limit` (default 50) | Conversation messages | -| `tools ` | `` | | Tool call stats + records (up to 30) | -| `context ` | `` | | Full context summary | -| `find ` | `` | | Search by keyword | -| `delete ` | `` | | Delete a session | -| `delete-smoke` | | | Bulk delete sessions with "smoke" in ID/title | +``` +/trae:sessions [options] +``` +
OpenCode +Call **trae-sessions** tool with parameters: + +| Parameter | Type | Required | Description | +|-----------|------|----------|-------------| +| `action` | enum | Yes | `list`, `detail`, `conversation`, `tools`, `context`, `recent`, `find`, `delete`, `delete-smoke` | +| `session_id` | string | No | 会话 ID（detail/conversation/tools/context/delete 需要） | +| `cwd` | string | No | 按工作目录筛选（list/recent） | +| `limit` | number | No | 返回数量限制，默认 20 | +| `topic` | string | No | 搜索关键词（find action） | +
+ +**Action reference:** + +| action | 需要 session_id | 描述 | +|--------|----------------|------| +| `list` | No | 列出所有会话 | +| `recent` | No | 最近一次会话 | +| `detail` | Yes | 查看会话元数据 + 事件计数 | +| `conversation` | Yes | 查看对话消息 | +| `tools` | Yes | 查看工具调用统计 + 记录（最多30条） | +| `context` | Yes | 查看完整上下文摘要 | +| `find` | No | 按关键词搜索（使用 `topic` 参数） | +| `delete` | Yes | 删除一个会话 | +| `delete-smoke` | No | 批量删除 ID/title 中包含 "smoke" 的会话 | + --- -### `acp ` +### `acp` ACP protocol: **JSON-RPC over STDIO** (not HTTP). Enables Agent-to-Agent communication. -
Claude Code/trae:acp [options]
OpenCodetrae-acp tool with `action` + args
+
Claude Code -| Action | Args | Description | -|--------|------|-------------| -| `start` | `--yolo`, `--allowed-tool`, `--disabled-tool` | Start ACP Server (blocks, keeps STDIO pipe open) | -| `stop` | | Stop ACP Server | -| `status` | | Check server status | -| `agents` | | Discover available agents (auto-starts server if needed) | -| `run ` | `` | Execute task via ACP (auto-starts server) | -| `stream ` | `` | Stream task output in real-time (auto-starts server) | +``` +/trae:acp [options] +``` +
OpenCode +Call **trae-acp** tool with parameters: + +| Parameter | Type | Required | Description | +|-----------|------|----------|-------------| +| `action` | enum | Yes | `start`, `stop`, `status`, `agents`, `run`, `stream` | +| `prompt` | string | No | 任务描述（run/stream 需要） | +| `yolo` | boolean | No | YOLO 模式（start/run） | +| `allowed_tools` | string[] | No | 允许的工具（start） | +| `disabled_tools` | string[] | No | 禁用的工具（start） | +
+ > **Note**: `acp start` is a blocking call — it holds the process alive for the STDIO pipe. Use `agents`, `run`, or `stream` for interactive use (they auto-start the server). --- ### `status` List all background tasks (reads `.claude-trae-plugin/` for PID files). -
Claude Code/trae:status
OpenCodetrae-status tool
+
Claude Code +``` +/trae:status +``` +
OpenCode + +Call **trae-status** tool (no parameters) +
+ --- -### `result ` +### `result` Get output of a background task. -
Claude Code/trae:result
OpenCodetrae-result tool with `task_id`
+
Claude Code +``` +/trae:result +``` +
OpenCode + +Call **trae-result** tool with parameters: + +| Parameter | Type | Required | Description | +|-----------|------|----------|-------------| +| `task_id` | string | Yes | 后台任务 ID（时间戳） | +
+ --- -### `cancel ` +### `cancel` Force-kill a background task (SIGKILL). -
Claude Code/trae:cancel
OpenCodetrae-cancel tool with `task_id`
+
Claude Code +``` +/trae:cancel +``` +
OpenCode + +Call **trae-cancel** tool with parameters: + +| Parameter | Type | Required | Description | +|-----------|------|----------|-------------| +| `task_id` | string | Yes | 要取消的后台任务 ID（时间戳） | +
+ --- ### `rescue` Diagnose recent task failures, collect error logs and git status. -
Claude Code/trae:rescue [--context text]
OpenCodetrae-rescue tool with optional `context`
+
Claude Code -| Option | Description | -|--------|-------------| -| `--context ` | Additional context for diagnosis | +``` +/trae:rescue [--context text] +``` +
OpenCode +Call **trae-rescue** tool with parameters: + +| Parameter | Type | Required | Description | +|-----------|------|----------|-------------| +| `context` | string | No | 提供额外的上下文信息帮助诊断 | +
+ --- ### Lifecycle Hooks Auto-triggered — no manual invocation needed. | Hook | Trigger | Behavior | |------|---------|----------| | `SessionStart` | Claude Code startup / OpenCode session.created | Check trae-cli, remind background tasks | | `SessionEnd` | Claude Code exit / OpenCode session.deleted | Clean old logs, remind running tasks | | `Stop` | User stops operation | Gate on uncommitted changes | | `PostToolUse` | After `/trae:review` | Log review results | --- ### `hooks ` (internal) Internal hook handler — invoked by lifecycle scripts, not users. ```bash trae-plugin-cc hooks session-start|session-end|stop-gate ``` ## Project Structure ``` trae-plugin-cc/ ├── .claude-plugin/ # Claude Code plugin + marketplace manifest ├── commands/ # Slash command docs (*.md) ├── .mcp.json # 4 MCP tool definitions ├── hooks/hooks.json # Claude Code lifecycle hooks ├── scripts/ # Hook scripts (*.mjs) ├── src/ # Core CLI (TypeScript → dist/index.js) │ ├── index.ts # Entrypoint │ ├── commands/ # Command handlers │ └── utils/ # TraeExecutor, SessionReader, AuthBridge, AcpClient... ├── .opencode/ # OpenCode integration │ ├── tools/ # 9 Bun tools (*.ts) │ ├── commands/ # 10 command docs (*.md) │ ├── plugins/ # Event hooks │ └── package.json # OpenCode deps (@opencode-ai/plugin) └── tests/ # Jest tests ``` ## Development ```bash npm run build # esbuild → dist/index.js (run after ANY src/ change) npm test # jest (all tests) cd .opencode && npm install && cd .. # OpenCode deps (once) ``` ### Build After Pulling Updates When you pull new code from the repository: ```bash git pull npm run build # required if src/ files changed ``` > `npm install` also builds (via `postinstall`), so either command works after a `git pull`. ### ⚠️ Build Rule `npm run build` uses **esbuild** (bundled single file with shebang) — this is the **production entrypoint**. `npm run build:tsc` produces separate `.js` files for module imports but is not used at runtime. | Command | Output | Used By | |---------|--------|---------| | `npm run build` | `dist/index.js` (bundled) | **Runtime CLI** | | `npm run build:tsc` | `dist/**/*.js` (separate) | Direct module imports | **Always run `npm run build` after modifying `src/` before testing.** This applies to OpenCode too — its tools spawn `dist/index.js`. ## Session Cache Paths trae-cli stores sessions on disk (read directly by this plugin): | Platform | Path | |----------|------| | macOS | `~/Library/Caches/trae_cli/sessions/` | | Linux | `~/.cache/trae_cli/sessions/` | ## License MIT

## 2. package.json

**Path:** `/Users/ubuntu/1-project/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc/package.json`

- **Name:** `trae-plugin-cc`, version `1.0.0`
- **Description:** Bridges Claude Code and OpenCode with ByteDance Trae Agent (`trae-cli`)
- **Main entry:** `dist/index.js`
- **Bin:** `trae-plugin-cc` -> `dist/index.js`
- **Scripts:**
  - `build`: esbuild bundles `src/index.ts` into `dist/index.js` with shebang, external Node modules
  - `build:tsc`: tsc only (rarely needed)
  - `postinstall`: auto-triggers `npm run build`
  - `test`: runs jest
- **Dependencies:** `js-yaml` only
- **DevDependencies:** `@types/jest`, `@types/js-yaml`, `@types/node`, `esbuild`, `jest`, `ts-jest`, `typescript`
- **Repository:** github.com/wangxumarshall/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc
- **License:** MIT

---

## 3. ALL TYPESCRIPT SOURCE FILES IN src/

### 3.1 Entry Point: `src/index.ts`
- **Role:** Single CLI entrypoint. Parses `process.argv[2]` and dispatches to command handlers.
- **Commands handled:** `setup`, `review`, `adversarial-review`, `run`, `status`, `result`, `cancel`, `hooks`, `rescue`, `sessions`, `acp`
- **Error handling:** Catches all errors and logs via `getErrorMessage()`

### 3.2 Config: `src/config.ts`
- **Constants:** `PLUGIN_DIR_NAME = '.claude-trae-plugin'`
- **Functions:** `getPluginDir()` (cwd-relative), `getCliCacheDirs()` (macOS/Linux paths), `getTraeCliConfigPath()` (~/.trae/trae_cli.yaml)

### 3.3 Utils Barrel: `src/utils.ts`
- Re-exports all utility classes: `SessionReader`, `AuthBridge`, `ContextBridge`, `TraeExecutor`, `AcpClient`, `AcpServerManager`
- Provides `isTraeCliInstalled()` (via `which trae-cli`) and `getGitDiff(baseBranch)` (with fallback to unstaged)
- Provides `ensurePluginDir()`

### 3.4 Commands

#### `src/commands/setup.ts` (51 lines)
Checks trae-cli installation + auth status via `AuthBridge`. Displays model, login URL, allowed tools, and plugins.

#### `src/commands/run.ts` (117 lines)
Parses CLI args for task execution: `--background`, `--json`, `--yolo`, `--resume`, `--session-id`, `--worktree`, `--allowed-tool`, `--disallowed-tool`, `--query-timeout`, `--bash-tool-timeout`, `-c`, `--inject-context`. Delegates to `TraeExecutor.execute()`.

#### `src/commands/review.ts` (84 lines)
Gets git diff, auto-detects base branch, estimates review size (and auto-enables background for large diffs), then submits to TraeExecutor. Supports `--base`, `--background`, `--yolo`, `--json`, `--session-id`.

#### `src/commands/sessions.ts` (309 lines)
9 actions: `list`, `detail`, `conversation`, `tools`, `context`, `recent`, `find`, `delete`, `delete-smoke`. Reads trae-cli's on-disk session cache directly.

#### `src/commands/acp.ts` (228 lines)
6 actions: `start`, `stop`, `status`, `agents`, `run`, `stream`. Uses `AcpServerManager` to spawn `trae-cli acp serve`, then `AcpClient` for JSON-RPC over STDIO.

#### `src/commands/jobs.ts` (100 lines)
Background job management: `getJobs()` (reads .pid files, checks process liveness), `status()`, `result()` (reads .log files), `cancel()` (SIGKILL).

#### `src/commands/rescue.ts` (110 lines)
Collects last error log, git status, recent commits, then submits diagnostic prompt to `TraeExecutor` for AI analysis.

#### `src/commands/hooks.ts` (46 lines)
Routes internal hook invocations (`session-start`, `session-end`, `stop-gate`) to corresponding `.mjs` scripts in `scripts/`.

### 3.5 Utilities

#### `src/utils/trae-executor.ts` (217 lines)
Core execution engine. `TraeExecutor.execute()` spawns `trae-cli` as child process. Supports foreground (with 5-min timeout, stdout/stderr capture, JSON parsing) and background (detached, PID file tracking, log file writing) modes. Builds CLI args from `TraeTaskConfig`.

#### `src/utils/session-reader.ts` (306 lines)
Reads trae-cli's on-disk session data from `~/Library/Caches/trae_cli/sessions/` or `~/.cache/trae_cli/sessions/`. Methods: `listSessions()`, `getSession()`, `getEvents()`, `getConversation()`, `getToolCalls()`, `getFileTrackStatus()`, `getRecentSession()`, `findSessionByTopic()`, `getContextSummary()`, `getHistory()`, `deleteSession()`.

#### `src/utils/auth-bridge.ts` (88 lines)
Reads and parses `~/.trae/trae_cli.yaml` (YAML). Provides: `loadConfig()`, `checkAuthStatus()`, `getLoginBaseUrl()`, `getModelName()`, `getAllowedTools()`, `getPlugins()`, `buildSpawnEnv()`.

#### `src/utils/context-bridge.ts` (99 lines)
Builds context from prior sessions for injection. Methods: `buildResumedPrompt()`, `findSessionByCwd()`, `findSessionByTopic()`, `buildContextFromSession()`, `injectContextToPrompt()`.

#### `src/utils/acp-client.ts` (216 lines)
JSON-RPC 2.0 client over STDIO. Manages message ID counter, pending request map with 60s timeouts, update callbacks. Methods: `initialize()`, `createSession()`, `loadSession()`, `sessionPrompt()`, `sessionCancel()`, `getSessionId()`. Parses newline-delimited JSON from stdout.

#### `src/utils/acp-server-manager.ts` (171 lines)
Manages `trae-cli acp serve` child process lifecycle. Uses pipe STDIO. Has 10s startup timeout with diagnostic tracing. Methods: `start()`, `stop()` (SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 3s grace), `isRunning()`, `getClient()`, `getStatus()`.

#### `src/utils/branch-detection.ts` (188 lines)
Git utilities: `detectBaseBranch()` (checks upstream/remote/fallback), `getUntrackedFiles()`, `estimateReviewSize()` (lines added/deleted, files changed, time estimate), `formatEstimate()` (human-readable output).

#### `src/utils/env.ts` (21 lines)
`buildSpawnEnv()` prepends `~/.local/bin` to PATH and forwards `TRAECLI_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`. `isInPath()` checks if a directory is already in PATH.

### 3.6 Types

#### `src/types/errors.ts` (39 lines)
Custom error classes: `TraeCliError`, `AuthError`, `ExecutionError`, `SessionNotFoundError`. Utility: `isError()`, `getErrorMessage()`.

## 6. MCP CONFIG (.mcp.json)

**Path:** `/Users/ubuntu/1-project/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc/.mcp.json`

4 MCP tools defined with JSON Schema parameters:

3. **`trae_sessions`** - Session management (action enum: list/detail/conversation/tools/context/recent/find/delete; optional: session_id, cwd, limit, topic)
4. **`trae_acp`** - ACP protocol (action enum: start/stop/status/agents/run/stream; optional: prompt, yolo, allowed_tools, disabled_tools)

## 7. HOOKS (hooks/hooks.json)

**Path:** `/Users/ubuntu/1-project/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc/hooks/hooks.json`

4 lifecycle hooks:
- **SessionStart** (matcher: "startup") -> `session-lifecycle-hook.mjs SessionStart` (timeout: 10s)
- **SessionEnd** -> `session-lifecycle-hook.mjs SessionEnd` (timeout: 10s)
- **Stop** -> `stop-review-gate-hook.mjs` (timeout: 60s)
- **PostToolUse** (matcher: regex `.*trae:review.*`) -> `session-lifecycle-hook.mjs PostReview` (timeout: 5s)

All use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` variable substitution.

## 8. SCRIPTS IN scripts/

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `session-lifecycle-hook.mjs` | 154 | Handles SessionStart (check trae-cli, remind running jobs), SessionEnd (cleanup stale 7-day logs, remind running tasks), PostReview (append to reviews.jsonl) |
| `stop-review-gate-hook.mjs` | 61 | Checks for uncommitted git changes + running jobs. Exits with code 1 to block session stop. `--force` bypasses. |
| `job-utils.mjs` | 41 | Shared utilities: `getPluginDir()`, `getRunningJobs()` (reads .pid files, `process.kill(pid, 0)` check), `isJobRunning()` |
| `trae-companion.mjs` | 131 | Alternate unified CLI entry point with JSON output mode (`--json`). Handles setup, estimate, status directly; delegates other commands to dist/index.js |

---

## 9. ALL OPENCODE TOOLS IN .opencode/tools/

All spawn `node dist/index.js` from the plugin root directory.

| Tool | Lines | Maps to CLI | Args |
|------|-------|-------------|------|
| `trae-setup.ts` | 19 | `setup` | None |
| `trae-run.ts` | 71 | `run` | prompt, resume, session_id, yolo, allowed_tools, disallowed_tools, json_output, background, worktree, query_timeout, bash_tool_timeout, inject_context |

| `trae-sessions.ts` | 55 | `sessions` | action (9 enums), session_id, cwd, limit, topic |
| `trae-acp.ts` | 45 | `acp` | action (6 enums), prompt, yolo, allowed_tools, disabled_tools |

| `trae-cancel.ts` | 22 | `cancel` | task_id (required) |
| `trae-rescue.ts` | 26 | `rescue` | context (optional) |

All use `@opencode-ai/plugin` SDK with `tool()` factory and Zod schemas for argument validation.

---

## 10. ALL OPENCODE COMMANDS IN .opencode/commands/

10 markdown files with YAML frontmatter (`description:` only). English language. Serve as agent-readable reference for CLI argument formats.

| File | Maps to |

| `trae-run.md` | `run` command with examples |

---

## 11. ALL OPENCODE PLUGINS IN .opencode/plugins/

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `trae-hooks.ts` | 108 | Maps OpenCode events to trae lifecycle hooks. `session.created` -> SessionStart (async), `session.deleted` -> SessionEnd (sync), `server.instance.disposed` -> SessionEnd (sync, fallback). Uses `Bun.spawn`/`Bun.spawnSync` to call the same `.mjs` scripts. JSON payloads via stdin. State tracked via `currentSessionID` closure variable. Failures silently caught. |
| `entire.ts` | 177 | Auto-generated by `entire enable --agent opencode`. Separate plugin for "Entire" CLI lifecycle logging. Covers session.created, message.updated, message.part.updated (turn-start), session.status (turn-end), session.compacted, session.deleted, server.instance.disposed. 1: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from 'child_process';
2: import * as fs from 'fs';
3: import * as path from 'path';
4: import { AuthBridge } from './auth-bridge';
5: import { getPluginDir } from '../config';
6: 
7: export interface TraeTaskConfig {
8:   prompt: string;
9:   background?: boolean;
10:   jsonOutput?: boolean;
11:   yolo?: boolean;
12:   allowedTools?: string[];
13:   disallowedTools?: string[];
14:   sessionId?: string;
15:   resume?: string;
16:   worktree?: string;
17:   queryTimeout?: string;
18:   bashToolTimeout?: string;
19:   configOverrides?: Record<string, string>;
20: }
21: 
22: export interface TraeTaskResult {
23:   taskId: string;
24:   output: string;
25:   exitCode: number | null;
26:   sessionId?: string;
27:   duration: number;
28:   jsonOutput?: any;
29: }
30: 
31: const PLUGIN_DIR = getPluginDir();
32: 
33: function ensurePluginDir() {
34:   if (!fs.existsSync(PLUGIN_DIR)) {
35:     fs.mkdirSync(PLUGIN_DIR, { recursive: true });
36:   }
37: }
38: 
39: export class TraeExecutor {
40:   private authBridge: AuthBridge;
41: 
42:   constructor() {
43:     this.authBridge = new AuthBridge();
44:   }
45: 
46:   async execute(config: TraeTaskConfig): Promise<TraeTaskResult> {
47:     ensurePluginDir();
48: 
49:     const taskId = Date.now().toString();
50:     const logFile = path.join(PLUGIN_DIR, `${taskId}.log`);
51:     const pidFile = path.join(PLUGIN_DIR, `${taskId}.pid`);
52:     const args = this.buildArgs(config);
53:     const env = this.authBridge.buildSpawnEnv();
54:     const startTime = Date.now();
55: 
56:     if (config.background) {
57:       return this.executeBackground(args, env, taskId, logFile, pidFile, startTime);
58:     }
59: 
60:     return this.executeForeground(args, env, taskId, logFile, pidFile, startTime, config.jsonOutput);
61:   }
62: 
63:   private buildArgs(config: TraeTaskConfig): string[] {
64:     const args: string[] = [];
65: 
66:     if (config.allowedTools) {
67:       for (const tool of config.allowedTools) {
68:         args.push('--allowed-tool', tool);
69:       }
70:     }
71: 
72:     if (config.disallowedTools) {
73:       for (const tool of config.disallowedTools) {
74:         args.push('--disallowed-tool', tool);
75:       }
76:     }
77: 
78:     if (config.yolo) args.push('--yolo');
79:     if (config.queryTimeout) args.push('--query-timeout', config.queryTimeout);
80:     if (config.bashToolTimeout) args.push('--bash-tool-timeout', config.bashToolTimeout);
81:     if (config.sessionId) args.push('--session-id', config.sessionId);
82:     if (config.resume) {
83:       if (config.resume === 'AUTO') {
84:         args.push('--resume');
85:       } else {
86:         args.push('--resume', config.resume);
87:       }
88:     }
89:     if (config.worktree) {
90:       if (config.worktree === '__auto__') {
91:         args.push('--worktree');
92:       } else {
93:         args.push('--worktree', config.worktree);
94:       }
95:     }
96: 
97:     if (config.configOverrides) {
98:       for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(config.configOverrides)) {
99:         args.push('-c', `${k}=${v}`);
100:       }
101:     }
102: 
103:     args.push('--print');
104: 
105:     if (config.jsonOutput) {
106:       args.push('--json');
107:     }
108: 
109:     args.push(config.prompt);
110: 
111:     return args;
112:   }
113: 
114:   private executeBackground(
115:     args: string[], env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv, taskId: string,
116:     logFile: string, pidFile: string, startTime: number
117:   ): TraeTaskResult {
118:     const out = fs.openSync(logFile, 'a');
119:     const err = fs.openSync(logFile, 'a');
120: 
121:     const child = spawn('trae-cli', args, {
122:       detached: true,
123:       stdio: ['ignore', out, err],
124:       env,
125:     });
126: 
127:     child.unref();
128: 
129:     if (child.pid) {
130:       fs.writeFileSync(pidFile, child.pid.toString());
131:     }
132: 
133:     return {
134:       taskId,
135:       output: `任务已在后台启动 (ID: ${taskId})\n日志文件: ${logFile}`,
136:       exitCode: null,
137:       duration: Date.now() - startTime,
138:     };
139:   }
140: 
141:   private executeForeground(
142:     args: string[], env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv, taskId: string,
143:     logFile: string, pidFile: string, startTime: number,
144:     parseJson: boolean = false
145:   ): Promise<TraeTaskResult> {
146:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
147:       const child = spawn('trae-cli', args, {
148:         stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
149:         env,
150:       });
151: 
152:       if (child.pid) {
153:         fs.writeFileSync(pidFile, child.pid.toString());
154:       }
155: 
156:       let combinedOutput = '';
157:       let settled = false;
158: 
159:       const append = (chunk: Buffer) => {
160:         const text = chunk.toString();
161:         combinedOutput += text;
162:         fs.appendFileSync(logFile, text);
163:       };
164: 
165:       const settle = (result: TraeTaskResult) => {
166:         if (settled) return;
167:         settled = true;
168:         if (fs.existsSync(pidFile)) fs.unlinkSync(pidFile);
169:         resolve(result);
170:       };
171: 
172:       const fail = (error: Error) => {
173:         if (settled) return;
174:         settled = true;
175:         if (fs.existsSync(pidFile)) fs.unlinkSync(pidFile);
176:         child.kill('SIGKILL');
177:         reject(error);
178:       };
179: 
180:       const TIMEOUT_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;
181:       const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
182:         fail(new Error(`任务执行超时 (300s)`));
183:       }, TIMEOUT_MS);
184: 
185:       child.stdout?.on('data', append);
186:       child.stderr?.on('data', append);
187: 
188:       child.on('error', (error) => {
189:         clearTimeout(timeout);
190:         fail(new Error(`执行失败: ${error.message}`));
191:       });
192: 
193:       child.on('close', (code) => {
194:         clearTimeout(timeout);
195: 
196:         let jsonOutput: any = undefined;
197:         let sessionId: string | undefined;
198: 
199:         if (parseJson && combinedOutput.trim()) {
200:           try {
201:             jsonOutput = JSON.parse(combinedOutput);
202:             sessionId = jsonOutput?.session_id;
203:           } catch {}
204:         }
205: 
206:         settle({
207:           taskId,
208:           output: combinedOutput,
209:           exitCode: code,
210:           sessionId,
211:           duration: Date.now() - startTime,
212:           jsonOutput,
213:         });
214:       });
215:     });
216:   }
217: }

(End of file - total 217 lines)
</content>

19: export interface SessionEvent {
20:   id: string;
21:   session_id: string;
22:   branch: string;
23:   agent_id: string;
24:   agent_name: string;
25:   parent_tool_call_id: string;
26:   created_at: string;
27:   message?: {
28:     message: {
29:       role: string;
30:       content: string | Array<{ type: string; text: string }>;
31:       tool_calls?: Array<{
32:         id: string;
33:         type: string;
34:         function: { name: string; arguments: string };
35:       }>;
36:     };
37:   };
38:   tool_call?: {
39:     tool_call_id: string;
40:     tool_info: {
41:       name: string;
42:       description: string;
43:       inputSchema: any;
44:     };
45:     input: any;
46:     is_programmatic?: boolean;
47:   };
48:   tool_call_output?: {
49:     tool_call_id: string;
50:     output: any;
51:     is_error?: boolean;
52:   };
53:   state_update?: {
54:     updates: Record<string, any>;
55:   };
56:   agent_start?: Record<string, any>;
57: }
58: 
59: export interface ConversationMessage {
60:   role: string;
61:   content: string;
62:   toolCalls?: string[];
63:   timestamp: string;
64: }
65: 
66: export interface ToolCallRecord {
67:   id: string;
68:   name: string;
69:   input: any;
70:   output?: any;
71:   isError?: boolean;
72:   timestamp: string;
73: }
74: 
75: export interface FileTrackStatus {
76:   [filePath: string]: {
77:     read_time: string;
78:     content: string;
79:   };
80: }

83:   session_id: string;
84:   messages?: any[];
85:   [key: string]: any;
86: }

88: export class SessionReader {
89:   private sessionsDir: string;
90:   private historyFile: string;
91: 
92:   constructor() {
93:     const candidates = getCliCacheDirs();
94:     const cacheDir = candidates.find(dir => fs.existsSync(dir)) || candidates[0];
95: 
96:     this.sessionsDir = path.join(cacheDir, 'sessions');
97:     this.historyFile = path.join(cacheDir, 'history.jsonl');
98:   }
99: 
100:   listSessions(options?: { cwd?: string; limit?: number }): SessionMeta[] {
101:     if (!fs.existsSync(this.sessionsDir)) return [];
102: 
103:     const sessions = fs.readdirSync(this.sessionsDir)
104:       .filter(dir => {
105:         const sessionFile = path.join(this.sessionsDir, dir, 'session.json');
106:         return fs.existsSync(sessionFile);
107:       })
108:       .map(dir => {
109:         try {
110:           const content = fs.readFileSync(
111:             path.join(this.sessionsDir, dir, 'session.json'),
112:             'utf-8'
113:           );
114:           return JSON.parse(content) as SessionMeta;
115:         } catch {
116:           return null;
117:         }
118:       })
119:       .filter((s): s is SessionMeta => s !== null)
120:       .sort((a, b) =>
121:         new Date(b.updated_at).getTime() - new Date(a.updated_at).getTime()
122:       );
123: 
124:     let filtered = sessions;
125:     if (options?.cwd) {
126:       filtered = filtered.filter(s => s.metadata.cwd === options.cwd);
127:     }
128: 
129:     if (options?.limit) {
130:       filtered = filtered.slice(0, options.limit);
131:     }
132: 
133:     return filtered;
134:   }
135: 
136:   getSession(sessionId: string): SessionMeta | null {
137:     const filePath = path.join(this.sessionsDir, sessionId, 'session.json');
138:     if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return null;
139:     try {
140:       return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8'));
141:     } catch {
142:       return null;
143:     }
144:   }
145: 
146:   getEvents(sessionId: string): SessionEvent[] {
147:     const filePath = path.join(this.sessionsDir, sessionId, 'events.jsonl');
148:     if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return [];
149: 
150:     const lines = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8').split('\n');
151:     return lines
152:       .filter(line => line.trim())
153:       .map(line => {
154:         try { return JSON.parse(line) as SessionEvent; }
155:         catch { return null; }
156:       })
157:       .filter((e): e is SessionEvent => e !== null);
158:   }
159: 
160:   getConversation(sessionId: string, options?: { limit?: number }): ConversationMessage[] {
161:     const events = this.getEvents(sessionId);
162:     let messages = events
163:       .filter(e => e.message)
164:       .map(e => {
165:         const msg = e.message!.message;
166:         let content = '';
167:         if (typeof msg.content === 'string') {
168:           content = msg.content;
169:         } else if (Array.isArray(msg.content)) {
170:           content = msg.content.map(c => c.text || '').join('\n');
171:         }
172: 
173:         return {
174:           role: msg.role,
175:           content,
176:           toolCalls: msg.tool_calls?.map(tc => tc.function.name),
177:           timestamp: e.created_at,
178:         };
179:       });
180: 
181:     if (options?.limit) {
182:       messages = messages.slice(-options.limit);
183:     }
184: 
185:     return messages;
186:   }
187: 
188:   getToolCalls(sessionId: string): ToolCallRecord[] {
189:     const events = this.getEvents(sessionId);
190:     const callMap = new Map<string, ToolCallRecord>();
191: 
192:     for (const e of events) {
193:       if (e.tool_call) {
194:         callMap.set(e.tool_call.tool_call_id, {
195:           id: e.tool_call.tool_call_id,
196:           name: e.tool_call.tool_info.name,
197:           input: e.tool_call.input,
198:           timestamp: e.created_at,
199:         });
200:       }
201:     }
202: 
203:     for (const e of events) {
204:       if (e.tool_call_output) {
205:         const call = callMap.get(e.tool_call_output.tool_call_id);
206:         if (call) {
207:           call.output = e.tool_call_output.output;
208:           call.isError = e.tool_call_output.is_error;
209:         }
210:       }
211:     }
212: 
213:     return Array.from(callMap.values());
214:   }
215: 
216:   getFileTrackStatus(sessionId: string): FileTrackStatus {
217:     const events = this.getEvents(sessionId);
218:     for (const e of events) {
219:       if (e.state_update?.updates?.file_track_status) {
220:         return e.state_update.updates.file_track_status;
221:       }
222:     }
223:     return {};
224:   }
225: 
226:   getRecentSession(cwd?: string): SessionMeta | null {
227:     const sessions = this.listSessions({ cwd });
228:     return sessions[0] || null;
229:   }
230: 
231:   findSessionByTopic(topic: string): SessionMeta | null {
232:     const sessions = this.listSessions();
233:     const match = sessions.find(s =>
234:       s.metadata.title.toLowerCase().includes(topic.toLowerCase())
235:     );
236:     return match || null;
237:   }
238: 
239:   getContextSummary(sessionId: string): string {
240:     const meta = this.getSession(sessionId);
241:     if (!meta) return '会话不存在';
242: 
243:     const conversation = this.getConversation(sessionId, { limit: 20 });
244:     const toolCalls = this.getToolCalls(sessionId);
245: 
246:     let summary = `## 会话: ${meta.metadata.title}\n`;
247:     summary += `- ID: ${meta.id}\n`;
248:     summary += `- 工作目录: ${meta.metadata.cwd}\n`;
249:     summary += `- 模型: ${meta.metadata.model_name}\n`;
250:     summary += `- 权限模式: ${meta.metadata.permission_mode}\n`;
251:     summary += `- 创建时间: ${meta.created_at}\n`;
252:     summary += `- 更新时间: ${meta.updated_at}\n\n`;
253: 
254:     summary += `### 最近对话 (${conversation.length} 条消息)\n`;
255:     for (const msg of conversation) {
256:       const content = msg.content.length > 200
257:         ? msg.content.substring(0, 200) + '...'
258:         : msg.content;
259:       summary += `**${msg.role}**: ${content}`;
260:       if (msg.toolCalls?.length) {
261:         summary += ` [调用工具: ${msg.toolCalls.join(', ')}]`;
262:       }
263:       summary += '\n\n';
264:     }
265: 
266:     summary += `### 工具调用统计 (${toolCalls.length} 次)\n`;
267:     const toolStats: Record<string, number> = {};
268:     for (const tc of toolCalls) {
269:       toolStats[tc.name] = (toolStats[tc.name] || 0) + 1;
270:     }
271:     for (const [name, count] of Object.entries(toolStats)) {
272:       summary += `- ${name}: ${count} 次\n`;
273:     }
274: 
275:     return summary;
276:   }
277: 
278:   getHistory(): Array<{ content: string; mode: string; timestamp: string }> {
279:     if (!fs.existsSync(this.historyFile)) return [];
280: 
281:     const lines = fs.readFileSync(this.historyFile, 'utf-8').split('\n');
282:     return lines
283:       .filter(line => line.trim())
284:       .map(line => {
285:         try { return JSON.parse(line); }
286:         catch { return null; }
287:       })
288:       .filter((h): h is { content: string; mode: string; timestamp: string } => h !== null);
289:   }
290: 
291:   deleteSession(sessionId: string): boolean {
292:     const sessionDir = path.join(this.sessionsDir, sessionId);
293:     if (!fs.existsSync(sessionDir)) return false;
294: 
295:     try {
296:       fs.rmSync(sessionDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
297:       return true;
298:     } catch {
299:       return false;
300:     }
301:   }
302: 
303:   getSessionsDir(): string {
304:     return this.sessionsDir;
305:   }
306: }

(End of file - total 306 lines)

1: import { Readable, Writable } from 'stream';
2: 
3: export interface AcpInitializeResult {
4:   protocolVersion: number;
5:   agentCapabilities: {
6:     _meta?: Record<string, any>;
7:     loadSession?: boolean;
8:     promptCapabilities?: Record<string, any>;
9:     mcpCapabilities?: { http: boolean; sse: boolean };

11:   };
12:   agentInfo?: { name: string; title: string; version: string };
13:   authMethods: any[];

15: }
16: 
17: export interface AcpSessionNewResult {
18:   sessionId: string;
19:   _meta?: Record<string, any>;
20:   models?: { availableModels: any[]; currentModelId: string };
21:   modes?: { availableModes: any[]; currentModeId: string };
22: }
23: 
24: export interface AcpSessionUpdate {
25:   sessionId: string;
26:   update: {
27:     sessionUpdate: string;
28:     content?: { type: string; text: string };
29:     [key: string]: any;
30:   };
31: }
32: 
33: export interface AcpSessionPromptResult {
34:   stopReason: string;
35:   [key: string]: any;
36: }
37: 
38: type PendingRequest = {
39:   resolve: (value: any) => void;
40:   reject: (error: Error) => void;
41: };
42: 
43: export class AcpClient {
44:   private stdin: Writable;
45:   private stdout: Readable;
46:   private stderr: Readable;
47:   private messageId = 0;
48:   private pendingRequests: Map<number, PendingRequest> = new Map();
49:   private requestTimeouts: Map<number, NodeJS.Timeout> = new Map();
50:   private initialized = false;
51:   private sessionId: string | null = null;
52:   private onUpdates: Array<(update: AcpSessionUpdate) => void> = [];
53:   private buffer = '';
54:   private readonly REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 60000;
55: 
56:   constructor(stdin: Writable, stdout: Readable, stderr: Readable) {
57:     this.stdin = stdin;
58:     this.stdout = stdout;
59:     this.stderr = stderr;
60: 
61:     const stderrChunks: string[] = [];
62:     stderr.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
63:       stderrChunks.push(chunk.toString());
64:     });
65: 
66:     this.stdout.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
67:       this.buffer += chunk.toString();
68:       const lines = this.buffer.split('\n');
69:       this.buffer = lines.pop() || '';
70:       for (const line of lines) {
71:         if (line.trim()) {
72:           this.handleMessages(line);
73:         }
74:       }
75:     });
76:   }
77: 
78:   async initialize(clientInfo = { name: 'trae-plugin-cc', version: '1.0.0' }): Promise<AcpInitializeResult> {
79:     if (this.initialized) {
80:       throw new Error('Already initialized');
81:     }
82: 
83:     const result = await this.request('initialize', {
84:       protocolVersion: 1,
85:       clientCapabilities: {},
86:       clientInfo,
87:     });
88: 
89:     this.initialized = true;
90:     return result;
91:   }
92: 
93:   async createSession(cwd: string, mcpServers: any[] = []): Promise<AcpSessionNewResult> {
94:     if (!this.initialized) {
95:       throw new Error('Not initialized. Call initialize() first');
96:     }
97: 
98:     const result = await this.request('session/new', {
99:       cwd,
100:       mcpServers,
101:     });
102: 
103:     this.sessionId = result.sessionId;
104:     return result;
105:   }
106: 
107:   async loadSession(sessionId: string, cwd: string, mcpServers: any[] = []): Promise<void> {
108:     if (!this.initialized) {
109:       throw new Error('Not initialized. Call initialize() first');
110:     }
111: 
112:     await this.request('session/load', {
113:       sessionId,
114:       cwd,
115:       mcpServers,
116:     });
117: 
118:     this.sessionId = sessionId;
119:   }
120: 
121:   async sessionPrompt(
122:     prompt: string,
123:     onUpdate?: (update: AcpSessionUpdate) => void
124:   ): Promise<AcpSessionPromptResult> {
125:     if (!this.sessionId) {
126:       throw new Error('No active session. Call createSession() or loadSession() first');
127:     }
128: 
129:     if (onUpdate) {
130:       this.onUpdates.push(onUpdate);
131:     }
132: 
133:     try {
134:       return await this.request('session/prompt', {
135:         sessionId: this.sessionId,
136:         prompt: [{ type: 'text', text: prompt }],
137:       });
138:     } finally {
139:       if (onUpdate) {
140:         this.onUpdates = this.onUpdates.filter((fn) => fn !== onUpdate);
141:       }
142:     }
143:   }
144: 
145:   async sessionCancel(): Promise<void> {
146:     if (!this.sessionId) {
147:       throw new Error('No active session');
148:     }
149: 
150:     await this.request('session/cancel', { sessionId: this.sessionId });
151:   }
152: 
153:   getSessionId(): string | null {
154:     return this.sessionId;
155:   }
156: 
157:   private handleMessages(line: string) {
158:     let message: any;
159:     try {
160:       message = JSON.parse(line);
161:     } catch {
162:       return;
163:     }
164: 
165:     if (message.id !== undefined && this.pendingRequests.has(message.id)) {
166:       const pending = this.pendingRequests.get(message.id)!;
167:       const timeout = this.requestTimeouts.get(message.id);
168:       if (timeout) {
169:         clearTimeout(timeout);
170:         this.requestTimeouts.delete(message.id);
171:       }
172:       this.pendingRequests.delete(message.id);
173: 
174:       if (message.error) {
175:         pending.reject(new Error(message.error.message || JSON.stringify(message.error)));
176:       } else {
177:         pending.resolve(message.result);
178:       }
179:     } else if (message.method === 'session/update' && message.params) {
180:       for (const fn of this.onUpdates) {
181:         fn(message.params);
182:       }
183:     }
184:   }
185: 
186:   private request(method: string, params: any): Promise<any> {
187:     const id = ++this.messageId;
188: 
189:     const message = {
190:       jsonrpc: '2.0',
191:       id,
192:       method,
193:       params,
194:     };
195: 
196:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
197:       const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
198:         this.pendingRequests.delete(id);
199:         this.requestTimeouts.delete(id);
200:         reject(new Error(`Request timeout: ${method} (${this.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS}ms)`));
201:       }, this.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS);
202: 
203:       this.pendingRequests.set(id, { resolve, reject });
204:       this.requestTimeouts.set(id, timeout);
205: 
206:       this.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(message) + '\n', (err) => {
207:         if (err) {
208:           clearTimeout(timeout);
209:           this.pendingRequests.delete(id);
210:           this.requestTimeouts.delete(id);
211:           reject(new Error(`Failed to write to stdin: ${err.message}`));
212:         }
213:       });
214:     });
215:   }
216: }

1: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from 'child_process';
2: import { AcpClient } from '../utils/acp-client';
3: import { buildSpawnEnv } from './env';
4: 
5: type StartupEvent = {
6:   at: string;
7:   event: string;
8:   detail?: string;
9: };
10: 
11: export class AcpServerManager {
12:   private process: ChildProcess | null = null;
13:   private client: AcpClient | null = null;
14: 
15:   async start(options?: { yolo?: boolean; allowedTools?: string[]; disabledTools?: string[] }): Promise<{
16:     client: AcpClient;
17:   }> {
18:     if (this.isRunning()) {
19:       return {
20:         client: this.client!,
21:       };
22:     }
23: 
24:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
25:       const args: string[] = ['acp', 'serve'];
26: 
27:       if (options?.yolo) args.push('--yolo');
28:       if (options?.allowedTools) {
29:         for (const tool of options.allowedTools) {
30:           args.push('--allowed-tool', tool);
31:         }
32:       }
33:       if (options?.disabledTools) {
34:         for (const tool of options.disabledTools) {
35:           args.push('--disabled-tool', tool);
36:         }
37:       }
38: 
39:       const startupEvents: StartupEvent[] = [];
40:       const outputSnippets: string[] = [];
41:       let started = false;
42:       let settled = false;
43: 
44:       const recordEvent = (event: string, detail?: string) => {
45:         startupEvents.push({
46:           at: new Date().toISOString(),
47:           event,
48:           detail,
49:         });
50:       };
51: 
52:       const rememberOutput = (source: 'stdout' | 'stderr', text: string) => {
53:         const normalized = text.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
54:         if (!normalized) return;
55:         outputSnippets.push(`[${source}] ${normalized}`);
56:         if (outputSnippets.length > 8) outputSnippets.shift();
57:       };
58: 
59:       const buildDiagnostic = () => {
60:         const trace = startupEvents
61:           .map((item, idx) => `${idx + 1}. ${item.at} ${item.event}${item.detail ? ` | ${item.detail}` : ''}`)
62:           .join('\n');
63:         const recentOutput = outputSnippets.length > 0
64:           ? outputSnippets.map((line, idx) => `${idx + 1}. ${line}`).join('\n')
65:           : 'none';
66:         return `\n[ACP] startup diagnostics\ntrace:\n${trace || 'none'}\nrecent_output:\n${recentOutput}`;
67:       };
68: 
69:       const fail = (message: string) => {
70:         if (settled) return;
71:         settled = true;
72:         reject(new Error(`${message}${buildDiagnostic()}`));
73:       };
74: 
75:       const succeed = () => {
76:         if (settled) return;
77:         settled = true;
78:         const client = new AcpClient(child.stdin!, child.stdout!, child.stderr!);
79:         this.process = child;
80:         this.client = client;
81:         recordEvent('stdio:ready');
82:         resolve({ client });
83:       };
84: 
85:       const env = buildSpawnEnv();
86: 
87:       recordEvent('spawn:start', `cmd=trae-cli ${args.join(' ')}`);
88: 
89:       // 关键修复：使用 pipe 而不是 ignore, 保持 stdin 打开
90:       // ACP 协议使用 STDIO JSON-RPC, 需要 stdin 保持连接
91:       const child = spawn('trae-cli', args, {
92:         stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
93:         env,
94:       });
95: 
96:       recordEvent('spawn:created', `pid=${child.pid || 'unknown'}`);
97: 
98:       child.stdout?.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
99:         rememberOutput('stdout', chunk.toString());
100:       });
101: 
102:       child.stderr?.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
103:         rememberOutput('stderr', chunk.toString());
104:       });
105: 
106:       child.on('error', (err) => {
107:         recordEvent('process:error', err.message);
108:         fail(`ACP server spawn failed: ${err.message}`);
109:       });
110: 
111:       child.on('close', (code, signal) => {
112:         recordEvent('process:close', `code=${code ?? 'null'}, signal=${signal ?? 'null'}`);
113:         this.process = null;
114:         if (!started) {
115:           fail(`ACP server exited before ready (code=${code ?? 'null'}, signal=${signal ?? 'null'})`);
116:         }
117:       });
118: 
119:       // 等待进程启动，认证过程可能需要较长时间
120:       setTimeout(() => {
121:         if (!started && !settled) {
122:           const alive = child.exitCode === null;
123:           recordEvent('startup:timeout', `alive=${alive}, exitCode=${child.exitCode ?? 'null'}`);
124: 
125:           if (alive) {
126:             started = true;
127:             succeed();
128:             return;
129:           }
130: 
131:           fail('ACP server startup timeout (15s)');
132:         }
133:       }, 10000);
134:     });
135:   }
136: 
137:   async stop(): Promise<void> {
138:     if (this.process) {
139:       this.process.stdin?.end();
140:       this.process.kill('SIGTERM');
141:       await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
142:         setTimeout(() => {
143:           if (this.process) {
144:             this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
145:           }
146:           resolve();
147:         }, 3000);
148:       });
149:       this.process = null;
150:       this.client = null;
151:     }
152:   }
153: 
154:   isRunning(): boolean {
155:     return this.process !== null && this.process.exitCode === null;
156:   }
157: 
158:   getClient(): AcpClient | null {
159:     return this.client;
160:   }
161: 
162:   getStatus(): {
163:     running: boolean;
164:     baseUrl: string;
165:   } {
166:     return {
167:       running: this.isRunning(),
168:       baseUrl: this.isRunning() ? 'stdio://trae-cli' : '',
169:     };
170:   }
171: }

(End of file - total 171 lines)

4: export async function handleHook(args: string[]) {
5:     const hookType = args[0];
6: 
7:     if (!hookType) {
8:         console.error('Usage: trae-plugin-cc hooks <session-start|session-end|stop-gate>');
9:         process.exit(1);
10:     }
11: 
12:     const hookMap: Record<string, { script: string; arg: string }> = {
13:         'session-start': { script: 'session-lifecycle-hook.mjs', arg: 'SessionStart' },
14:         'session-end': { script: 'session-lifecycle-hook.mjs', arg: 'SessionEnd' },
15:         'stop-gate': { script: 'stop-review-gate-hook.mjs', arg: '' },
16:     };
17: 
18:     const entry = hookMap[hookType];
19:     if (!entry) {
20:         console.error(`Unknown hook type: ${hookType}`);
21:         console.error(`Available: ${Object.keys(hookMap).join(', ')}`);
22:         process.exit(1);
23:     }
24: 
25:     const pluginRoot = process.env.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT || path.join(__dirname, '..');
26:     const scriptPath = path.join(pluginRoot, 'scripts', entry.script);
27: 
28:     const spawnArgs = entry.arg ? [scriptPath, entry.arg] : [scriptPath];
29: 
30:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
31:         const child = spawn('node', spawnArgs, {
32:             stdio: 'inherit',
33:             detached: false
34:         });
35: 
36:         child.on('close', (code) => {
37:             if (code === 0) {
38:                 resolve(undefined);
39:             } else {
40:                 reject(new Error(`Hook exited with code ${code}`));
41:             }
42:         });
43: 
44:         child.on('error', reject);
45:     });
46: }

3: import * as yaml from 'js-yaml';
4: import { buildSpawnEnv } from './env';
5: import { getTraeCliConfigPath } from '../config';
6: 
7: interface TraeCliConfig {
8:   model?: { name: string };
9:   allowed_tools?: string[];
10:   plugins?: Array<{ name: string; type: string; source: string; enabled: boolean }>;
11:   trae_login_base_url?: string;
12: }
13: 
14: export interface AuthStatus {
15:   authenticated: boolean;
16:   model: string;
17:   loginUrl: string;
18:   configPath: string;
19:   configExists: boolean;
20: }
21: 
22: export class AuthBridge {
23:   private configPath: string;
24:   private config: TraeCliConfig | null = null;
25: 
26:   constructor() {
27:     this.configPath = getTraeCliConfigPath();
28:   }
29: 
30:   loadConfig(): TraeCliConfig | null {
31:     if (this.config) return this.config;
32: 
33:     if (fs.existsSync(this.configPath)) {
34:       try {
35:         const content = fs.readFileSync(this.configPath, 'utf-8');
36:         this.config = yaml.load(content) as TraeCliConfig;
37:         return this.config;
38:       } catch {
39:         return null;
40:       }
41:     }
42:     return null;
43:   }
44: 
45:   async checkAuthStatus(): Promise<AuthStatus> {
46:     const config = this.loadConfig();
47:     let authenticated = false;
48: 
49:     try {
50:       execSync('trae-cli config edit --help', { stdio: 'ignore' });
51:       authenticated = true;
52:     } catch {
53:       authenticated = config !== null;
54:     }
55: 
56:     return {
57:       authenticated,
58:       model: config?.model?.name || 'unknown',
59:       loginUrl: config?.trae_login_base_url || 'https://console.enterprise.trae.cn',
60:       configPath: this.configPath,
61:       configExists: fs.existsSync(this.configPath),
62:     };
63:   }
64: 
65:   getLoginBaseUrl(): string {
66:     if (!this.config) this.loadConfig();
67:     return this.config?.trae_login_base_url || 'https://console.enterprise.trae.cn';
68:   }
69: 
70:   getModelName(): string {
71:     if (!this.config) this.loadConfig();
72:     return this.config?.model?.name || 'unknown';
73:   }
74: 
75:   getAllowedTools(): string[] {
76:     if (!this.config) this.loadConfig();
77:     return this.config?.allowed_tools || [];
78:   }
79: 
80:   getPlugins(): Array<{ name: string; type: string; source: string; enabled: boolean }> {
81:     if (!this.config) this.loadConfig();
82:     return this.config?.plugins || [];
83:   }
84: 
85:   buildSpawnEnv(): NodeJS.ProcessEnv {
86:     return buildSpawnEnv();
87:   }
88: }

1: import { execSync } from 'child_process';
2: 
3: export interface DiffEstimate {
4:   baseBranch: string;
5:   linesAdded: number;
6:   linesDeleted: number;
7:   filesChanged: number;
8:   untrackedFiles: string[];
9:   estimatedTime: 'quick' | 'moderate' | 'lengthy' | 'very_large';
10:   recommendation: {
11:     useBackground: boolean;
12:     reason: string;
13:   };
14: }
15: 
16: export async function detectBaseBranch(): Promise<string> {
17:   const commonBranches = ['main', 'master', 'develop', 'dev', 'mainline'];
18: 
19:   // Check current branch's upstream
20:   try {
21:     const upstream = execSync('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u}', {
22:       encoding: 'utf-8'
23:     }).trim();
24: 
25:     if (upstream) {
26:       const base = upstream.split('/').pop();
27:       if (base && commonBranches.includes(base.toLowerCase())) {
28:         return base;
29:       }
30:     }
31:   } catch {
32:     // Ignore
33:   }
34: 
35:   // Check if current branch is one of common branches
36:   try {
37:     const currentBranch = execSync('git branch --show-current', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
38:     if (commonBranches.includes(currentBranch.toLowerCase())) {
39:       return 'main';
40:     }
41:   } catch {
42:     // Ignore
43:   }
44: 
45:   // Try to find default branch from remote
46:   try {
47:     const remoteDefault = execSync('git remote show origin | grep "HEAD branch" | sed "s/.*: //"', {
48:       encoding: 'utf-8'
49:     }).trim();
50: 
51:     if (remoteDefault) {
52:       return remoteDefault;
53:     }
54:   } catch {
55:     // Ignore
56:   }
57: 
58:   // Fallback to common branches
59:   for (const branch of commonBranches) {
60:     try {
61:       execSync(`git rev-parse --verify ${branch}`, { stdio: 'ignore' });
62:       return branch;
63:     } catch {
64:       continue;
65:     }
66:   }
67: 
68:   return 'main';
69: }
70: 
71: export async function getUntrackedFiles(): Promise<string[]> {
72:   try {
73:     const output = execSync('git status --porcelain', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
74:     if (!output) return [];
75: 
76:     return output
77:       .split('\n')
78:       .filter(line => line.startsWith('??'))
79:       .map(line => line.substring(3).trim());
80:   } catch {
81:     return [];
82:   }
83: }
84: 
85: export async function estimateReviewSize(baseBranch: string): Promise<DiffEstimate> {
86:   let linesAdded = 0;
87:   let linesDeleted = 0;
88:   let filesChanged = 0;
89: 
90:   // Get diff stats - using fixed commands only
91:   try {
92:     const diffStats = execSync(`git diff --shortstat ${baseBranch}...HEAD`, {
93:       encoding: 'utf-8'
94:     }).trim();
95: 
96:     if (diffStats) {
97:       const match = diffStats.match(/(\d+)\s+files? changed.*?(\d+)\s+insertions.*?(\d+)\s+deletions/);
98:       if (match) {
99:         filesChanged = parseInt(match[1]) || 0;
100:         linesAdded = parseInt(match[2]) || 0;
101:         linesDeleted = parseInt(match[3]) || 0;
102:       }
103:     }
104:   } catch {
105:     // Fallback to unstaged
106:     try {
107:       const diffStats = execSync('git diff --shortstat', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
108:       if (diffStats) {
109:         const match = diffStats.match(/(\d+)\s+files? changed/);
110:         if (match) {
111:           filesChanged = parseInt(match[1]) || 0;
112:         }
113:       }
114:     } catch {
115:       // Ignore
116:     }
117:   }
118: 
119:   // Get untracked files
120:   const untrackedFiles = await getUntrackedFiles();
121:   const totalChanges = linesAdded + linesDeleted;
122: 
123:   // Determine estimated time
124:   let estimatedTime: DiffEstimate['estimatedTime'];
125:   if (totalChanges < 100) {
126:     estimatedTime = 'quick';
127:   } else if (totalChanges < 500) {
128:     estimatedTime = 'moderate';
129:   } else if (totalChanges < 2000) {
130:     estimatedTime = 'lengthy';
131:   } else {
132:     estimatedTime = 'very_large';
133:   }
134: 
135:   // Determine recommendation - based on tracked changes, not untracked
136:   let useBackground = false;
137:   let reason = '';
138: 
139:   if (totalChanges < 100) {
140:     useBackground = false;
141:     reason = '变更较小，可以同步等待';
142:   } else if (totalChanges < 500) {
143:     useBackground = true;
144:     reason = '变更适中，建议后台运行';
145:   } else if (totalChanges < 2000) {
146:     useBackground = true;
147:     reason = '变更较大，建议后台运行';
148:   } else {
149:     useBackground = true;
150:     reason = '变更非常大，建议后台运行或拆分审查';
151:   }
152: 
153:   return {
154:     baseBranch,
155:     linesAdded,
156:     linesDeleted,
157:     filesChanged,
158:     untrackedFiles,
159:     estimatedTime,
160:     recommendation: { useBackground, reason }
161:   };
162: }
163: 
164: export function formatEstimate(estimate: DiffEstimate): string {
165:   const lines = [
166:     '📊 审查估算:',
167:     `   基准分支: ${estimate.baseBranch}`,
168:     `   变更: +${estimate.linesAdded} -${estimate.linesDeleted} 行`,
169:     `   文件: ${estimate.filesChanged} 个`,
170:   ];
171: 
172:   if (estimate.untrackedFiles.length > 0) {
173:     lines.push(`   未跟踪: ${estimate.untrackedFiles.length} 个文件`);
174:   }
175: 
176:   const timeMap = {
177:     quick: '快速 (1-2分钟)',
178:     moderate: '中等 (5-10分钟)',
179:     lengthy: '较长 (10-30分钟)',
180:     very_large: '非常长 (30分钟+)'
181:   };
182: 
183:   lines.push(`   预计时间: ${timeMap[estimate.estimatedTime]}`);
184:   lines.push('');
185:   lines.push(`💡 建议: ${estimate.recommendation.reason}`);
186: 
187:   return lines.join('\n');
188: }

(End of file - total 188 lines)
</content>

1: import { SessionReader, SessionMeta } from './session-reader';
2: 
3: export interface ResumedPrompt {
4:   args: string[];
5:   contextPreview: string;
6: }
7: 
8: export class ContextBridge {
9:   private sessionReader: SessionReader;
10: 
11:   constructor() {
12:     this.sessionReader = new SessionReader();
13:   }
14: 
15:   buildResumedPrompt(
16:     sessionId: string,
17:     newTask: string
18:   ): ResumedPrompt {
19:     const context = this.sessionReader.getContextSummary(sessionId);
20:     const lastMessages = this.sessionReader.getConversation(sessionId, { limit: 5 });
21: 
22:     let contextPreview = `## 恢复会话 ${sessionId}\n\n`;
23:     contextPreview += `### 最近对话:\n`;
24:     for (const msg of lastMessages) {
25:       const preview = msg.content.length > 100
26:         ? msg.content.substring(0, 100) + '...'
27:         : msg.content;
28:       contextPreview += `- **${msg.role}**: ${preview}\n`;
29:     }
30: 
31:     return {
32:       args: ['--resume', sessionId, '--print', newTask],
33:       contextPreview,
34:     };
35:   }
36: 
37:   findSessionByCwd(cwd: string): string | null {
38:     const recent = this.sessionReader.getRecentSession(cwd);
39:     return recent?.id || null;
40:   }
41: 
42:   findSessionByTopic(topic: string): string | null {
43:     const match = this.sessionReader.findSessionByTopic(topic);
44:     return match?.id || null;
45:   }
46: 
47:   buildContextFromSession(sessionId: string): string {
48:     const meta = this.sessionReader.getSession(sessionId);
49:     if (!meta) return '';
50: 
51:     const conversation = this.sessionReader.getConversation(sessionId, { limit: 10 });
52:     const toolCalls = this.sessionReader.getToolCalls(sessionId);
53:     const fileStatus = this.sessionReader.getFileTrackStatus(sessionId);
54: 
55:     let context = `## Trae CLI 会话上下文\n\n`;
56:     context += `**会话标题**: ${meta.metadata.title}\n`;
57:     context += `**工作目录**: ${meta.metadata.cwd}\n`;
58:     context += `**模型**: ${meta.metadata.model_name}\n\n`;
59: 
60:     if (conversation.length > 0) {
61:       context += `### 对话摘要\n`;
62:       for (const msg of conversation) {
63:         const preview = msg.content.length > 150
64:           ? msg.content.substring(0, 150) + '...'
65:           : msg.content;
66:         context += `- **${msg.role}**: ${preview}\n`;
67:       }
68:       context += '\n';
69:     }
70: 
71:     if (toolCalls.length > 0) {
72:       context += `### 工具调用记录 (${toolCalls.length} 次)\n`;
73:       const toolNames = [...new Set(toolCalls.map(tc => tc.name))];
74:       for (const name of toolNames) {
75:         const count = toolCalls.filter(tc => tc.name === name).length;
76:         context += `- ${name}: ${count} 次\n`;
77:       }
78:       context += '\n';
79:     }
80: 
81:     const trackedFiles = Object.keys(fileStatus);
82:     if (trackedFiles.length > 0) {
83:       context += `### 已访问文件 (${trackedFiles.length} 个)\n`;
84:       for (const f of trackedFiles.slice(0, 20)) {
85:         context += `- ${f}\n`;
86:       }
87:       context += '\n';
88:     }
89: 
90:     return context;
91:   }
92: 
93:   injectContextToPrompt(sessionId: string, newPrompt: string): string {
94:     const context = this.buildContextFromSession(sessionId);
95:     if (!context) return newPrompt;
96: 
97:     return `${context}\n---\n\n基于以上上下文，请继续执行以下任务:\n\n${newPrompt}`;
98:   }
99: }
